---
title: "FFTW"
subtitle: "Fastest Fourier Transform in the West"
---

[FFTW](https://www.fftw.org) is a C library for computing discrete Fourier
transforms in one or more dimensions, of arbitrary input size, in both real and
complex data. It is a mature, widely used, high-performance FFT implementation.

**Version vendored:** 3.3.10 (`fftw/fftw-3.3.10/`).

## Role in fesm-utils

FFTW is a build-time dependency of downstream models and of **SHTns**, which
links against the FFTW built here for its spherical-harmonic transforms. Because
of that link dependency, FFTW must be installed **before** SHTns.

## Building

FFTW is built by its own autotools through the root `Makefile`:

```bash
make fftw openmp=0        # serial -> fftw/fftw-serial
make fftw openmp=1        # OpenMP -> fftw/fftw-omp
```

The target runs, in `fftw/fftw-3.3.10/`:

```bash
./configure --prefix=<repo>/fftw/fftw-<variant> --disable-doc [--enable-openmp]
make && make install
```

The serial and OpenMP variants install side by side into `fftw/fftw-serial` and
`fftw/fftw-omp`. Machine/compiler specifics (the C compiler pick, C-standard
pins) live in [`config/libs.mk`](https://github.com/fesmc/fesm-utils/blob/main/config/libs.mk).

## Using it downstream

Reference the install prefix directly:

```
fesm-utils/fftw/fftw-serial/include    # fftw3.h, fftw3.f03
fesm-utils/fftw/fftw-serial/lib        # libfftw3.a (+ libfftw3_omp for the omp variant)
```

## Updating the vendored source

To try a different upstream version, unpack it into the `fftw/` container folder
and rebuild:

```bash
wget https://www.fftw.org/fftw-3.3.10.tar.gz
tar -xvf fftw-3.3.10.tar.gz -C fftw/
rm fftw-3.3.10.tar.gz
make fftw openmp=0
```
